sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with anxiety about recent blood work showing a diagnosis of hepatitis C.
She reports feeling fine despite the diagnosis.
She denies any past medical history of hepatitis C, but admits to a history of intravenous drug use and alcohol consumption, with a current alcohol intake of one beer per day.
She also reports smoking one to two cigarettes per day.
She has a family history of hypertension, diabetes, and depression.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of intravenous drug use, which she has been clean from for over 15 years.
She has a history of heavy alcohol consumption, but currently consumes one beer per day.
She smokes one to two cigarettes per day.
Her family history includes hypertension, diabetes, and depression.
Review of systems: The patient reports feeling tired at the end of the day, despite getting enough sleep, but denies any other symptoms.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient appears in no distress and no jaundice on skin exam.
Her heart exam reveals a regular rate and rhythm with a 2/6 systolic murmur, which has not changed from last year.
Her lung exam is clear with no wheezes, rales, or rhonchi.
Her abdominal exam reveals present bowel sounds, a soft abdomen with no hepatosplenomegaly.
Her musculoskeletal exam is unremarkable.
Test Results: The patient's HCV antibody test was positive, and her liver panel showed an elevated AST at 39, but normal albumin and total bilirubin levels.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a confirmed diagnosis of hepatitis C, based on the positive HCV antibody test and elevated AST level.
Problem list: Hepatitis C; Hypertension; Diabetes; Depression.
Plan:
I prescribed [NOT MENTIONED].
I ordered additional blood work to confirm the diagnosis and determine the severity of the patient's liver disease, including HCV RNA and HCV genotype testing.
I also ordered an ultrasound to assess liver fibrosis.
I will schedule follow-up appointments in three weeks to discuss the results and determine the next steps in treatment.
I will also recommend that the patient's family members undergo screening for hepatitis C.
Follow-up: I will see the patient in three weeks to review the results of the additional testing and discuss the next steps in treatment.
